title: Best Herbal Teas for Excessive Sweating and Hot Temperatures
intro_desc: Herbal teas for excessive sweating are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of hyperhidrosis by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and regulating body temperature. The best herbal teas for excessive sweating include Hypericum perforatum, Zingiber officinale, Melissa officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Avena sativa, Sesamum indicum, Urtica dioica, Valeriana officinalis, and Melaleuca alternifolia. If left untreated, excessive sweating can severely impact daily life, leading to social anxiety, embarrassment, and difficulties in maintaining personal relationships and work performance, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tea is known for its ability to help with excessive sweating. The main reason for this is its natural ability to calm the nervous system. When our nervous system is calm, our body temperature and heart rate decrease, which in turn reduces sweat production. This is because the nervous system controls the sweat glands, and when it's relaxed, it doesn't send as many signals to these glands to produce sweat. Additionally, Melissa officinalis tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation that can occur as a result of excessive sweating. It also has a soothing effect on the body, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, common causes of excessive sweating. This tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to excessive sweating.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for excessive sweating because it has natural antiperspirant properties that help reduce sweat production and kill bacteria that cause body odor.
Apigenin is good for excessive sweating because it has antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce sweat production and soothe skin irritation caused by excessive sweating.

Luteolin is good for excessive sweating because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce body temperature and sweat production, thereby providing relief from excessive sweating.

remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for excessive sweating
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, you'll need dried Eucalyptus leaves, a pot, a strainer, and a cup. Start by boiling water in the pot and then add a tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus leaves for every cup of water. Let the leaves steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse into the water. Strain the leaves using the strainer and discard the leaves. Pour the tea into a cup and drink it while it's still warm. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink it within 30 minutes for maximum effectiveness. Drink 2-3 cups of this tea a day to help reduce excessive sweating. Note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy that can help with excessive sweating. The main reason for this is that ginger has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce sweating. When you drink ginger tea, the active compounds in the ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, are absorbed into your bloodstream and then travel to your sweat glands. Once there, they help to slow down the production of sweat, making you feel cooler and more comfortable. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating. This can be especially helpful for people who experience sweating due to anxiety or stress, as ginger has a calming effect on the nervous system. Furthermore, ginger tea has antiseptic properties that can help to prevent the growth of bacteria on the skin, reducing the risk of infection and body odor.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for excessive sweating because they help reduce the body's ability to produce sweat by increasing the nerve cells' sensitivity, which in turn helps to control the body's temperature regulation and sweat production.

Shogaols are good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the nervous system, which in turn reduces excessive sweating by regulating body temperature.

Curcuminoids are good for excessive sweating because they have natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce sweat gland inflammation and prevent infection, thereby alleviating excessive sweating.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for excessive sweating
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering dried eucalyptus leaves or purchasing eucalyptus tea bags. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then let it cool for a minute or two. Now, take one eucalyptus tea bag or add one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus leaves to a cup. Pour the boiling water over the eucalyptus, making sure the leaves are fully submerged. Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the eucalyptus oil to infuse into the water. After the steeping time is up, carefully remove the eucalyptus leaves or tea bag and discard them. Your Eucalyptus globulus tea is now ready to drink. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink it while it's still warm for maximum benefits. Drinking this herbal tea regularly can help reduce excessive sweating by its decongestant properties and its ability to soothe the body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St. John's Wort tea, is a natural remedy that may help with excessive sweating. This tea has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including anxiety and stress, which are common causes of excessive sweating. The tea contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help calm the nervous system, reduce anxiety, and promote relaxation, making it easier to control excessive sweating. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum tea may also help to regulate the body's temperature, which can be beneficial for people who experience hot flashes or night sweats. Drinking this tea may help to reduce the frequency and intensity of excessive sweating episodes, providing relief and comfort to those affected.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for excessive sweating because it has antiperspirant properties that help to reduce sweat production by blocking the receptors that stimulate sweat glands.
Hyperoside is good for excessive sweating because it has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body and help to reduce body temperature, thus alleviating symptoms of hyperhidrosis.
Kaempferol is good for excessive sweating because it helps to reduce inflammation and block the production of chemical signals that cause sweat glands to work too hard.

remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal teas for excessive sweating
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering dried eucalyptus leaves, which can be found in most herbal stores or online. Begin by boiling one liter of water in a pot, then add one to two teaspoons of dried eucalyptus leaves. Let it steep for about five to seven minutes, allowing the leaves to release their fragrance and medicinal properties into the water. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can then add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Once ready, drink the tea as needed, ideally one to two cups per day, to help alleviate excessive sweating caused by hyperhidrosis or other conditions. It's also recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using eucalyptus tea as a treatment, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tea is a natural remedy for excessive sweating. This is because it helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of excessive sweating. The tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help to lower body temperature and reduce sweat production. It also contains a compound called avenacoside, which can help to regulate the body's response to stress and heat. When we are under stress, our body's "fight or flight" response is triggered, leading to excessive sweating. Avena sativa tea helps to calm this response, making it easier for our body to regulate its temperature and sweat levels. Additionally, the tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and cool the skin. As a result, avena sativa tea can provide relief from excessive sweating, helping to keep you cool and comfortable.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for excessive sweating because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce skin irritation and soothe overheated nerve endings, thereby controlling sweat production.

Saponins are good for excessive sweating because they help reduce sweat production by soothing the nervous system and regulating body temperature, thus providing relief from excessive sweating and its associated symptoms.
Ferulic acid is good for excessive sweating because it helps to reduce sweat production and block the nerve signals that stimulate sweating, thereby providing relief from excessive sweating.
Phenolic acids are good for excessive sweating because they have natural antiperspirant and antibacterial properties that help to reduce sweat production and prevent bacterial growth on the skin.
Silicic acid helps with excessive sweating because it has a cooling and drying effect on the skin, reducing moisture and thereby minimizing sweat and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that can help with excessive sweating, start by gathering a few fresh or dried eucalyptus leaves. If you have access to fresh leaves, pluck them from the plant and rinse them with cold water to remove any dirt or debris. If you're using dried leaves, simply measure out one teaspoon for every cup of boiling water. Place the eucalyptus leaves in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, then pour in boiling water. Allow the leaves to steep for five to seven minutes, or until the water has cooled to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into a separate cup to remove the leaves, and enjoy the refreshing flavor. You can drink one to three cups per day, depending on your needs. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal teas, especially if you're experiencing excessive sweating due to a medical condition. Eucalyptus globulus tea has natural decongestant and antiseptic properties that may help to reduce sweating and promote a sense of calm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis tea, also known as soapwort tea, is a natural remedy that may help with excessive sweating. This is because it has a cooling effect on the body, which can help to reduce the temperature and decrease sweat production. The tea is made from the roots and stems of the saponaria plant, which contain a type of saponin that has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. When consumed, saponaria officinalis tea may help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which are common triggers for excessive sweating. It may also help to balance the body's natural temperature regulation, making it a potential natural remedy for people who experience sweating due to overheating. Additionally, saponaria officinalis tea has been traditionally used to treat a range of skin conditions, including eczema and acne, which may be related to excessive sweating. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin, saponaria officinalis tea may help to alleviate the symptoms of excessive sweating and promote overall skin health.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for excessive sweating because it helps to reduce inflammation and block the action of the hormone that stimulates sweat glands, thereby providing relief from excessive sweating.

Polyphenols are good for excessive sweating because they have natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help reduce body odor and inflammation, thereby minimizing sweat buildup and discomfort.

Tannins are good for excessive sweating because they have antiperspirant and astringent properties that help reduce sweat production and dry the skin, thereby providing relief from excessive sweating.
Flavonoids help reduce excessive sweating by calming the nervous system and balancing body temperature, which can help prevent overactive sweat glands.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves, a large pot, a strainer, and a cup. Begin by rinsing the Eucalyptus leaves with water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, fill the large pot with 2 cups of boiling water and add 1/2 cup of the Eucalyptus leaves. Allow the mixture to simmer for 5-10 minutes, releasing the medicinal properties of the Eucalyptus into the water. Remove the pot from the heat and let it steep for another 5 minutes. Strain the tea into your cup using the strainer, discarding the Eucalyptus leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to drink the tea plain to get the full benefits. Drink 2-3 cups of the Eucalyptus tea per day to help reduce excessive sweating and promote overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tea, also known as valerian root tea, may help with excessive sweating. The tea contains valerenic acid, which can calm the nervous system. When the nervous system is calm, the body doesn't feel the need to sweat as much. This is because excessive sweating is often a sign of an overactive nervous system, which can be caused by stress or anxiety. By calming the nervous system, valerian root tea can help reduce the amount of sweat produced. Additionally, valerian root tea has been traditionally used to treat sleep disorders, which can also be a cause of excessive sweating. By promoting better sleep, valerian root tea can help reduce stress and anxiety, leading to less sweating. The tea has also been known to have a cooling effect on the body, which can help reduce the temperature and sweat production.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for excessive sweating because it has antispasmodic properties that help to relax sweat glands and reduce the amount of sweat produced.

Valeranone is good for excessive sweating because it has a cooling effect on the body, reduces inflammation, and helps to balance the body's temperature regulation, thus providing relief from excessive sweating.
Valerenol is good for excessive sweating because it has a cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce the production of sweat and alleviate feelings of overheating and discomfort.

remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal teas for excessive sweating
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering a few fresh eucalyptus leaves from the garden or purchase dried eucalyptus leaves from a health food store. Rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, in a medium-sized pot, bring 2 cups of water to a boil. Add 1/4 cup of fresh eucalyptus leaves or 2 teaspoons of dried eucalyptus leaves to the boiling water. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. The eucalyptus leaves will release their oils and fragrance into the water, creating a soothing and refreshing tea. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Let the tea cool down for a few minutes before drinking. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of eucalyptus tea per day to help manage excessive sweating. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using eucalyptus tea as a treatment for any medical condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, has been used for centuries in many cultures to help with excessive sweating. The tea is made from the leaves and stems of the lemongrass plant, which contain compounds that help to reduce sweating. One of the main reasons for this is that lemongrass has a natural cooling effect on the body, which helps to lower body temperature and reduce sweating. This is because lemongrass contains oils that help to cool the body down, similar to how menthol or peppermint can make you feel cooler. Additionally, lemongrass tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excessive sweating. It also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to excessive sweating. Furthermore, lemongrass tea has been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections that can occur when sweat becomes trapped in the skin. By drinking lemongrass tea, people with excessive sweating may find that they sweat less, feel cooler, and experience fewer skin problems.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Limonene is good for excessive sweating because it has natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to reduce sweat-causing bacteria on the skin.
Linalool is good for excessive sweating because it has a cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce the production of sweat by acting as a natural antiperspirant.
Geraniol is good for excessive sweating because it helps to reduce sweat and has a cooling effect that can calm the skin and reduce discomfort caused by excessive sweating.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering a few fresh or dried eucalyptus leaves. If you're using fresh leaves, simply pluck a few sprigs from a healthy eucalyptus plant or purchase them from a local market. If you have dried leaves, you can skip this step. Next, boil a cup of water in a kettle or on the stove. Remove the water from the heat and add 2-3 sprigs of fresh eucalyptus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves to the hot water. Allow the leaves to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a light green color and has a refreshing aroma. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consume the tea hot, as cold tea may not be as effective in reducing excessive sweating. To enhance the benefits, you can drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally after waking up or before going to bed, and see how your body responds to the eucalyptus globulus herbal tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, has been used for centuries to help with excessive sweating. This is because the essential oils in the tea have a cooling effect on the body. The main compound responsible for this effect is linalool, which helps to reduce the production of sweat. When consumed as a tea, Lavandula angustifolia can help to regulate body temperature, making it easier to manage hot flashes and night sweats. Additionally, the tea has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and itchiness caused by excessive sweating. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Lavandula angustifolia tea can also help to alleviate stress-related sweating.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea to help with excessive sweating, you will need dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves and a tea infuser or a saucepan. Start by boiling water in a kettle or on the stove, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves to the tea infuser or directly to the boiling water in the saucepan. Allow the leaves to steep for 5-7 minutes to release their oils and flavor. You can adjust the steeping time according to your preference for the tea's strength. Once the leaves have steeped, carefully pour the tea into a cup, being careful not to spill any of the hot liquid. Strain the tea if using loose leaves and discard the leaves. Your Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea is now ready to drink. To help with excessive sweating, consume 1-2 cups of the tea a day, ideally after meals or when you feel the need to sweat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tea, also known as milk thistle tea, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help with excessive sweating. The tea is made from the seeds of the Silybum marianum plant, which is rich in flavonoids and silymarin. Silymarin is an anti-inflammatory compound that can help to soothe and calm the nervous system, which is often responsible for excessive sweating. When taken as a tea, the flavonoids in Silybum marianum can help to regulate body temperature and reduce the production of sweat. This can be especially helpful for people who suffer from hot flashes or night sweats. Additionally, Silybum marianum tea may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for excessive sweating. By drinking Silybum marianum tea regularly, some people have reported a reduction in their sweating and an improvement in their overall comfort levels.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried Eucalyptus leaves. If using fresh leaves, rinse them under cold running water and pat them dry with a clean cloth. If using dried leaves, simply measure out one teaspoon for every 250ml of water. Next, heat one cup of water in a saucepan over low heat, then add the Eucalyptus leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the water has turned a light greenish color and the leaves have released their fragrance. Remove the leaves from the water and let it cool down for a few minutes before straining it into a cup. You can add a squeeze of lemon juice and a bit of honey to taste, but be sure to drink it while it's still warm as the heat from the tea can help to open up your pores and promote sweating. Drinking this tea regularly, especially after meals or before bedtime, can help to reduce excessive sweating and leave your skin feeling refreshed and revitalized.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural remedy for excessive sweating. The main ingredient, eucalyptol, has a cooling effect on the body. It helps to reduce the production of sweat glands, which can cause excessive sweating. Eucalyptol also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin. Excessive sweating can be caused by stress, anxiety, or an overactive thyroid gland, and eucalyptus globulus tea can help to alleviate these underlying causes. When consumed as a tea, eucalyptus globulus can help to regulate body temperature, making it easier to manage excessive sweating. The tea also has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent bacterial growth on the skin, reducing the risk of infections and body odor. Drinking eucalyptus globulus tea regularly may help to reduce sweat production and alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating.

remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for excessive sweating
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal tea that helps with excessive sweating, start by gathering a handful of fresh eucalyptus leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried eucalyptus leaves. If you're using fresh leaves, be sure to dry them in a low-temperature oven or let them air dry to remove excess moisture. Next, boil a cup of water in a saucepan and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add the eucalyptus leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their medicinal properties. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and discard the eucalyptus leaves. Finally, enjoy the tea hot or iced, and repeat the process as needed to help alleviate excessive sweating. It's also essential to note that you can combine eucalyptus with other herbs like peppermint or lemongrass to create a more refreshing and invigorating tea.

supplementary_best: To remedy excessive sweating, a combination of herbal teas from 'Lavandula angustifolia', 'Avena sativa', and 'Cymbopogon citratus' is recommended. Lavender tea, made from 'Lavandula angustifolia', has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce stress and anxiety that can cause excessive sweating. Avena sativa tea, also known as oat straw tea, is high in silica, which helps to strengthen skin and improve circulation, reducing the appearance of excessive sweating. Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, has antiseptic and antifungal properties that can help to prevent infection and reduce inflammation associated with excessive sweating. The combination of these teas helps to address both the physical and emotional aspects of excessive sweating, making it a well-rounded remedy.
supplementary_avoid: If you have excessive sweating, you should avoid certain herbal teas that can make this condition worse. One such herb is 'Echinacea'. Echinacea is known to stimulate the immune system and increase heart rate. This can cause more sweat production, making excessive sweating worse.  Another herb to avoid is 'Ginkgo biloba'. Ginkgo biloba is believed to increase blood flow and heart rate, which can lead to excessive sweating. Some people may also experience anxiety and stress due to Ginkgo biloba, and excessive sweating can be a symptom of these conditions.  'Peppermint' tea may also not be suitable for people with excessive sweating. While peppermint can help with digestion and relaxation, it can also cause stomach cramps and increased heart rate in some individuals. This may lead to more sweat production.
supplementary_other_remedies: Excessive sweating can be uncomfortable and embarrassing. Besides herbal teas, there are other natural remedies that can help. Apple cider vinegar, for example, has been shown to help reduce sweat production. Its acidity can help balance the body's pH levels, which in turn can help slow down sweat glands. Some people also use baking soda to absorb moisture and reduce sweat. When mixed with water, baking soda forms a paste that can be applied to the skin to help draw out sweat. Another option is to use coconut oil, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help prevent skin infections that can be caused by excessive sweating. Some people also use essential oils like tea tree oil and lavender oil, which have natural antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help prevent skin infections. However, it's essential to note that these remedies may not work for everyone, and it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before trying new treatments.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, often experience other health issues. One common problem is anxiety and stress, which can trigger excessive sweating. This is because the same nervous system that controls sweat glands also controls the body's response to stress. The herb ashwagandha, an adaptogen, helps the body manage stress and anxiety by regulating the nervous system.  Another issue is an imbalance of gut bacteria, which can lead to digestive problems like bloating and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Probiotics, like the herb bacopa monnieri, can help restore a healthy balance of gut bacteria. This can reduce excessive sweating by reducing stress and anxiety, and also improving digestion.  Excessive sweating can also be linked to an underactive thyroid gland, also known as hypothyroidism. This is because the thyroid gland regulates metabolism, which in turn affects sweat production. The herb blumea camphora, also known as white camphor, has been traditionally used to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve metabolism.  Some people with excessive sweating may also experience low blood sugar or hypoglycemia, which can cause sweating. The herb gymnema sylvestre can help regulate blood sugar levels and reduce excessive sweating.  Lastly, excessive sweating can be a sign of an underlying infection, such as a skin infection or a fungal infection like ringworm. The herb tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial properties, can help reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi, thus reducing excessive sweating.
